description_long: This is a vintage military seaplane with a biplane wing configuration, painted in a matte gray finish with prominent roundel insignias on the upper wings and tail. The aircraft features two large floats beneath the fuselage for water landings, and a single propeller engine mounted at the nose. The tail is adorned with red, white, and blue stripes, and the fuselage displays white alphanumeric markings. The cockpit is positioned forward and appears to be enclosed, with a clear canopy visible. The overall design is reminiscent of early to mid-20th-century reconnaissance or patrol floatplanes, with robust struts supporting the wings and floats.
description: A vintage gray military seaplane with biplane wings, large floats for water landings, and colorful roundel insignias on the wings and tail.
description_view_0: Rear three-quarter view showing the twin floats, tail with tricolor stripes, and roundel insignias on the upper wings.
description_view_1: Direct rear view highlighting the full wingspan, roundels on both wings, and the symmetry of the floats.
description_view_2: Opposite rear three-quarter view, similar to view 0 but from the other side, showing the propeller and cockpit placement.
description_view_3: Direct front view displaying the propeller, cockpit canopy, and the alignment of the floats beneath the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane with straight wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Twin fixed floats for water landings, no visible wheels.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
